page: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 64 virtual CPUs (32 physical cores with hyperthreading) operating at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 2400 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate a balanced profile with notable strengths in specific multi-threaded and cryptographic workloads. While single-core CPU performance in stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10%, multi-core execution and memory bandwidth fall within the middle 50% average. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions and encryption, which rank in the top 25%. Additionally, database operations and Redis SET operations, achieving 56.86 million operations per second, perform in the top 25%, reflecting efficient data processing. Ray tracing execution via Geekbench also ranks in the top 25%, whereas large-scale LLM text generation is weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5ad.16xlarge offers a high-density compute profile with integrated high-speed local NVMe storage, reducing the need for external block storage for temporary data. The combination of dedicated AMD EPYC cores and local SSDs makes this instance highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, and high-throughput web serving. It is well-suited for workloads such as software compilation, cryptography tasks, and caching layers like Redis, though it is less optimal for single-threaded applications or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 20 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance but strong multi-core execution, with Passmark CPU encryption, database operations, and Redis SET operations ranking in the top 25%. The instance provides a cost-effective balance of high-density compute and fast local storage, making it highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, software compilation, and caching layers.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 2400 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong database and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge features 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2400 GB NVMe SSD storage for compute-heavy and database workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance combining sixty-four AMD EPYC virtual cores with high-speed local NVMe storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 64 vCPUs and 128 GB DDR4 RAM
Bundled with 2400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Strong performance in database operations, cryptography, and Redis caching
Optimal for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and web serving
